refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and task scheduling to improve energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors integrated with telemetry systems for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to regulate power usage based on load and terrain.
DP3: Use centralized task scheduling and optimization software to plan operations minimizing energy per ton.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring (DP1) and control (DP2)
Coordination needed between control (DP2) and optimization (DP3)
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable with clear interface definitions; modular separation supports maintainability.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity for telemetry.
Risk of control algorithm inefficiency under unexpected conditions.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of operational data and task models.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure robust sensor calibration and fault detection; validate control algorithms under diverse scenarios; incorporate feedback loops in optimization.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system integration including vehicle sensors, control software, and optimization modules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time data exchange between vehicle sensors and central control/optimization modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational tasks and schedules from optimization software.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts if energy efficiency control actions conflict with safety constraints.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as all DPs are integrated here; low diffusion risk due to centralized architecture.
Contains sensors and control actuators but limited optimization logic; moderate diffusion risk if control logic is duplicated.
Minimal containment; mostly remote control and monitoring; higher diffusion risk if energy control logic spreads here.
Safety alerts interface only; no direct containment of energy efficiency logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing energy efficiency functions within NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and supports integrated verification.
containment_risks: Potential for control logic duplication if Vehicle module implements partial control independently.
Interface complexity between NGHE Solution and Safety Services for alert coordination.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and ownership boundaries to prevent logic duplication; formalize alert protocols with Safety Services.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched with selected DPs addressing monitoring, control, and optimization aspects of energy efficiency.

diffusion_risks: Risk of control logic duplication between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Risk of interface complexity with Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Centralized containment in NGHE Solution module is appropriate; requires strict interface management.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and energy_per_ton KPI measurement; ownership boundaries support traceability.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Strengthen interface definitions to avoid diffusion.
Include fault detection and fallback strategies in control DP.
Coordinate with Safety Services for alert handling.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirements with manageable risks and clear ownership; no further decomposition needed at this stage.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and task scheduling to improve energy efficiency.

revise.why: To refine interface contracts and prevent logic duplication between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.

assumptions: Effective integration of control and optimization algorithms.
evidence_needed: Control algorithm performance under operational conditions.
Interface compliance testing with Safety Services.
